examples: If an ant finds an outstanding hole—such as the inside of an acorn or a rock crevice—it recruits another scout to check it out.

This crevice, the hiatus of a gulf of mire, was called a fontis, in the special tongue.
The company grew quickly and became like water on pavement, finding every crack and crevice in the online space and finding purchase.
